core_identity.title: Using Inverse Trigonometric Ratios to Find Missing Angles
core_identity.author: Gerry Docherty

curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: inverse trig ratios to find a missing angle
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Use inverse sine, cosine, and tangent to calculate unknown angles in right-angled triangles from two known sides, using correct calculator settings and clear reasoning.

verse_ality.driving_question: What is always true about inverse trig functions, and how is finding an angle different from finding a side?

knowing_world.focus: Work backwards from side ratios to find a missing angle in a right-angled triangle using sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, or tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, choosing the correct inverse function and interpreting calculator output in degrees.
key_concepts: Opposite, adjacent, hypotenuse (relative to the chosen angle)
Choosing the correct ratio from the known sides

human_story.focus: Explore why inverse trig matters in real work: surveyors and engineers often know two sides and must calculate an angle precisely to design ramps, slopes, and bridges without relying on protractors.
justice_prompts: Where do we need to be exact with angles? Who benefits from this skill?

expressive_self.focus: Make personal meaning of inverse trig by explaining it in your own way and creating scenarios where finding an angle matters, noticing what feels tricky and what supports your confidence.
activities: Make a poster explaining how to use sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, and tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ to find angles.
Create your own real-life triangle scenario (e.g., leaning ladder, ramp angle) and describe what you would need to measure.
Reflect: What part of using the calculator feels tricky? What helps?
Explain inverse trig to someone who has never seen it before (your choice of format).

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive sketch to generate triangles, hide/reveal values, and practise calculating missing angles with inverse trig‚ÄĒthen evaluate how to design learning tools that are accessible beyond visuals.
design_challenges: Challenge yourself or a partner: hide two sides and calculate the missing angle.
Use a calculator and one inverse trig ratio (sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, or tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ) that matches the known sides.
Reveal the true value and check your accuracy; correct errors and try again.
Drag point C to generate new triangles and build fluency across many cases.
Ethical design: How could this angle-finder work for someone using a screen reader or without sight?

regulation_supports: Stretch your arms open wide like triangle arms.
Look up toward the top angle and breathe in for 4, out for 6.
Sit upright like a triangle‚Äôs vertical side and relax your hands.
wellbeing_prompts: How does your body feel after solving something tricky?

pathways.name: Final Task: Trig Detective (SDG 4 ‚Äď Quality Education)
pathways.focus: Create and solve triangle ‚Äėblueprints‚Äô where the angle labels have been scrambled, using inverse trig to calculate and justify the correct missing angles.
products: Three triangle scenarios (with different known side pairs).
For each scenario: working, final angle answer, and which inverse function you used (sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, or tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ).
Screenshots (or equivalent evidence) and a short reflection.
Self-assessment using the GCSE Maths Rubric: Conceptual Understanding, Fluency & Accuracy, Application to Problems, Independence & Reflection.

required: Identify opposite, adjacent and hypotenuse relative to a chosen angle in a right-angled triangle
Use a calculator confidently in degree mode
Understand basic trig ratios (sin, cos, tan) and what they represent

builds_toward: Problem solving with angles of elevation and depression
